sections.paragraph:
m. (from the root שָׁכַב) pl. ־ִים constr. ־ֵי Lev 18:22., and ֹות Psa 149:5.

(1) a lying down, both for sleep ( 2Sa 4:5, מִשְׁכַּב הַצָּהֳרַיִם “sleep at noon”) and from sickness, Psa 41:4. Specially concubitus, Lev 18:22, “thou shalt not lie with a male מִשְׁכְּבֵי־אִשָּׁה the lying with a woman,” i.e. as with a woman; Lev 20:13 Num 31:17, 18 Num 31:18, 35 Num 31:35.

(2) a couch, a bed, 2Sa 17:28 a bier, 2Ch 16:14 Isa 57:2.
